hypotheses.title: Thermal Stress Melatonin Surge in Symbiodiniaceae
hypotheses.mechanism_summary: Conserved thermal melatonin surge (10-100x) in Symbiodiniaceae paralleling plant SNAT/ASMT upregulation under heat stress; foundational measurement never performed
hypotheses.self_critique_notes: No scavenging claimed; plant surge is grounded; dinoflagellate prediction is speculative but directly testable by HPLC-MS/MS

hypotheses.title: Melatonin-Enhanced NPQ as Thermal PSII Shield
hypotheses.mechanism_summary: Roopin 2013 NPQ enhancement reinterpreted as protective during thermal stress; melatonin-driven VDE/PsbS upregulation reduces PSII excitation pressure below D1 damage threshold
hypotheses.self_critique_notes: NPQ mechanism, not scavenging; reinterpretation of Roopin 2013 ambient data to thermal context is genuinely novel

hypotheses.title: AFMK Cascade Amplification Under Thermal ROS Surge
hypotheses.mechanism_summary: Thermal ROS accelerates melatonin-AFMK-AMK cascade flux via IDO and non-enzymatic oxidation; 10x per-molecule amplification but still only 0.4% of OH flux
hypotheses.self_critique_notes: CAUTION: even 10x cascade yields 0.4% OH scavenging. Retained for diagnostic metabolite profiling value, not as primary protection mechanism

hypotheses.title: Melatonin-Mediated Antioxidant Enzyme Induction in Symbiodiniaceae
hypotheses.mechanism_summary: Melatonin at nM concentrations upregulates SOD/CAT/APX transcription in plants; predict same signaling-based enzyme induction in dinoflagellates despite absent receptor
hypotheses.self_critique_notes: Enzyme induction is signaling, not scavenging; plant mechanism is well-grounded; absence of receptor in dinoflagellates explicitly acknowledged

hypotheses.title: Durusdinium vs Cladocopium Melatonin Differential Underlies Thermal Tolerance
hypotheses.mechanism_summary: Thermally tolerant Durusdinium predicted to have higher baseline melatonin and greater thermal surge than sensitive Cladocopium; no comparative profiling exists post-taxonomic revision
hypotheses.self_critique_notes: Comparative profiling gap is genuine; melatonin differential is one of many possible tolerance factors

hypotheses.title: SNAT/AANAT Transcriptional Upregulation as Thermal Stress Biomarker
hypotheses.mechanism_summary: SNAT thermal induction (3-8 fold in plants) predicted in Symbiodiniaceae; testable by mining existing PRJNA723630 transcriptome data
hypotheses.self_critique_notes: Existing transcriptome data provides immediate testability; dinoflagellate post-transcriptional regulation caveat noted

hypotheses.title: Exogenous Melatonin Delays Bleaching in Intact Coral Holobionts
hypotheses.mechanism_summary: Bath application of 50 microM melatonin to coral fragments delays bleaching via NPQ enhancement and enzyme induction; translational from plant agronomy
hypotheses.self_critique_notes: Zeaxanthin precedent validates concept; supraphysiological concentration acknowledged but consistent with plant application doses

hypotheses.title: Nocturnal Melatonin Peak as Thermal Damage Recovery Window
hypotheses.mechanism_summary: Roopin 2013 nocturnal melatonin peak creates temporal D1 repair window; nighttime melatonin enhances PSII recovery from daytime thermal photodamage
hypotheses.self_critique_notes: Nocturnal peak is grounded; D1 repair connection is novel and testable via lincomycin assay; confounds in constant light experiment noted

hypotheses.title: Melatonin as Symbiosome-Crossing Retrograde Signal During Thermal Stress
hypotheses.mechanism_summary: Lipophilic melatonin diffuses across symbiosome membrane during thermal stress surge; modulates host NF-kB/caspase bleaching response without requiring receptor
hypotheses.self_critique_notes: Most speculative mechanistic hypothesis; symbiosome crossing is physically plausible (logP 1.6); host vs symbiont origin separation is technically challenging

hypotheses.title: Coral Probiotic Strategy — Melatonin-Producing Bacteria for Bleaching Mitigation
hypotheses.mechanism_summary: Select/engineer coral-associated bacteria that overproduce melatonin as BMC probiotic; sustained melatonin supply enhances symbiont thermal tolerance
hypotheses.self_critique_notes: BMC concept and bacterial melatonin production both grounded; multi-barrier delivery and colonization stability are key unknowns; spawning disruption risk noted

self_critique_caveats: H3: cascade amplification yields only 0.4% OH scavenging even with 10x amplification — retained for diagnostic value only
constraints_applied: Roopin 2013 (PMID 23496383) cited in 6/10 hypotheses, referenced in remaining 4
No direct ROS scavenging as primary mechanism — all hypotheses use indirect mechanisms
Indirect mechanisms used: NPQ enhancement, enzyme induction, AFMK cascade, D1 repair, retrograde signaling, BMC delivery
No melatonin receptor fabricated in dinoflagellates — absence explicitly acknowledged in H4, H9
Novel angle (thermal stress + bleaching) maintained across all 10 hypotheses
bridge_types_used: Indirect enzymatic cascade (H2, H3, H4, H8, H9)
